title: Stakeholder Outcomes, Owner Authorization, Ethics, and Evidence · Contract Lens: Stakeholder Decision
summary: A request to audit a public website names pages to collect but no owner, user decision, permitted purpose, stop contact, privacy boundary, or proof that findings are useful. Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler ts stakeholder decision increment in authorized inclusive site-audit charter. Learners progress through pre…

Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate useful owner-authorized, privacy-minimized, inclusive crawl outcomes and claim layers; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Define stakeholder, affected users, decision, site population, success, failure harm, and observable value before choosing crawler features.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Stakeholder Decision model · Theory Stakeholder…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Downloading many pages is itself a valuable stakeholder outcome.
